behaviors.description: Missing required key from both YAML and `TOOL_` env vars surfaces an actionable error naming the exact config path (for example `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This failure sits at the boundary where file loading, env-overlay, schema validation, and error formatting interact. A unit test of validation alone can miss real failures in merge order or path rendering.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp config directory, write a valid YAML missing `database.host`; clear related `TOOL_` vars; run the real loader startup path (not mocked parser/merger). Assert it fails and the error text contains the exact missing path `database.host` (full dotted path, exact spelling).

behaviors.description: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` overrides default config root resolution (instead of `~/.config`).
behaviors.justification: Real risk is incorrect wiring between environment lookup and filesystem path resolution. Pure function mocks can hide path-construction mistakes, while no subprocess/network is required.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory and create config only under that location. Ensure no config exists under a fake/default home path. Execute real loader path resolution and assert it reads `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml` successfully.

behaviors.description: When file and `TOOL_` environment values conflict, environment value wins.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence logic across two real config sources. Mocking one source can mask merge-order bugs that only appear when both are parsed and combined.
behaviors.test_description: Create YAML with `database.host: file-host`; set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`; run real loader merge flow. Assert resulting config uses `env-host` and no fallback/file value remains for that key.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors report an error message including the detected line number.
behaviors.justification: Line-number fidelity depends on the real YAML parser output propagated through loader error handling. Mocked parser exceptions often hide formatting/propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML with a known parse failure line in temp config file; invoke real loader with real YAML parser. Assert failure message contains a numeric line reference matching parser-reported location.

behaviors.description: If config directory is absent on startup, loader creates it and writes defaults before continuing.
behaviors.justification: This is filesystem side-effect behavior across directory creation, file write, and subsequent load. In-process mocks can miss path, permissions, or ordering bugs in real local I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Point config root to a fresh temp location that does not exist; run startup loader. Assert directory now exists, defaults file was written with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds using that file (no failure).
